issues.title: Provide a way to bulk-initialize a log
issues.author: Bren2010
issues.body:
There are two aspects to this:

1. We want to be able to add a certain number of fake entries to hide the actual size of the log.
2. We want to be able to add pre-existing key-value pairs without having one log entry per pair (like we would for key-value pairs added after log creation).

It should be very efficient to have a large number of fake prefix tree entries. We also want to not leak the number of real entries that a log starts with

issues.title: Prefix tree should commit to sets of versions
issues.author: felixlinker
issues.body: Currently, the prefix tree commits to one key-version pair per leaf. I would argue that the leafs should commit to lists of key-version pairs (as the Merkle^2 paper suggests). This way, users can more easily monitor that the server did not include a fake key before to the current epoch.

comments.author: Bren2010
comments.body: I'm not sure there's any security benefit to having multiple key-version pairs per leaf. It makes things more complicated though, because you have to add an extra step where you are digging into the multiple changes that happened at that one leaf

comments.author: felixlinker
comments.body: Brendan and I clarified this issue in a call. My understanding of which indices are used in the prefix tree was wrong.

pulls.title: Improvements to hash computation definitions
pulls.author: felixlinker
pulls.body:
Hi Brendan,

Two suggestions.

1. I made the recursion on the hash computation more explicit and gave also leaves the value field. I think this is clearer. The only downside is that you might need to store more information on disk, but the data structures will likely not be implemented like this anyways, no?
2. I dropped that cryptographic computations must be done with the TLS presentation language, because in the algorithms, you reference individual `opaque` fields only anyways. Will this change with the definition of other primitives? Could be. But so far, I thought that this sentence would only falsely lead people to believe that they must use TLS datastructures, ASN.1 parsers, etc., in the library whereas my understanding is that they need not do that.

Cheers!

Felix

comments.author: felixlinker
comments.body: Brendan and I discussed this PR in a call. I missed that the TLS data-structures to which I added the `value` field are used elsewhere in cryptographic computations.

Crypto computations do indeed need to be done in TLS format. TLS specifies endianness, that `opaque` fields are length-prefixed byte strings, etc. Not related to ASN.1. See: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5246#section-4
I don't think it makes sense to store the `value` in the leaf node, as the value can be immediately computed from `key_version`. So it is redundant when this struct is used later on.

The reason to explicitly say `parent.value = ` is that `value` is an actual field of `PrefixParent` that needs to be populated, potentially stored on-disk. That connection is kind of lost when you roll it into `nodeValue`. And unlike `PrefixLeaf`, `value` can't be computed in isolation

Is this the level that this spec will operate on? What is stored on disk and what not? This seems like implementation-level details we should not address. Rather, we should address the logic behind the system and have the pseudocode be as understandable as possible. So if leafs have a concept of *value* associated with them, I find it clearer to be explicit about that. Implementors can then apply all nifty tricks to get the size on disk down as much as possible.

pulls.title: More intuition on tree construction
pulls.author: felixlinker
pulls.body: With this PR, I try to give a more high-level intuition of what happens in KT, but there is a discrepancy between what I write here (and the Merkle^2 paper) and what you write. I will open an issue separately for that.

comments.body:
Maintains two things:
- A log of changes to lookup keys
- A set of key-version pairs

comments.author: Bren2010
comments.body: Feel free to add an organization as well if you'd like
